saveframes.name: entry_information
tags: Solid-state Chemical Shift Assignments for p150Glued CAP-Gly Domain in complex with EB1 at 19.9 T
The chemical shifts reported here are from the U-13C,U-15N CAP-Gly binding to natural abundance EB1.

data: Using the same NMR experiment conditions, instrument and reference. From the chemical shift difference between the current entry and 19025, we can know the chemical shift perturbations of CAP-Gly upon binding to EB1

saveframes.name: entry_citation
tags: Three-Dimensional Structure of CAP-Gly Domain of Mammalian Dynactin Determined by Magic Angle Spinning NMR Spectroscopy: Conformational Plasticity and Interactions with End-Binding Protein EB1.

saveframes.name: assembly
tags: The EB1c forms homodimer first. Then, by adding the p150Gluded CAP-Gly, two molecules of CAP-Gly bind to the EB1c homodimer.

saveframes.name: CAP-Gly
tags: The sequence starts from S19, and ends at E107. Residues S19-P26 and E98-E107 are not assigned due to the dynamics of the termini.
CAP-Gly domain of p150Glued subunit of dynactin
